topic: Cloudflare Project Think Guide 2026: Build Long-Running AI Agents with Durable Execution

key_points: Official Cloudflare blog: Project Think is a set of new primitives for building long-running agents
Introduces Fibers for durable execution — checkpoints the instruction pointer to SQLite
Sub-agents (Facets) are child Durable Objects colocated with the parent
Session API stores conversations as a relational tree with parent_id for branching
Sandboxed code execution via Dynamic Workers (V8 isolates)
@cloudflare/think is an opinionated base class available in preview
InfoQ analysis: Think transitions AI agents from stateless orchestration to durable actor-based infrastructure
AWS Bedrock AgentCore and Google ADK use request-response model with externalized KV state
Cloudflare kernel-like runtime: agents survive platform restarts
Relational memory trees managed inside co-located SQLite
Self-authored extensions: agents create new tools at runtime via ExtensionManager
Project Think announced during Cloudflare Agents Week April 2026
Combines durable execution, sub-agents, sandboxed code execution, persistent sessions
Long-running reasoning models (taking minutes) work out of the box
Agents can schedule tasks on delay, at specific time, or on a cron schedule
Agents can wake themselves up, do work, and go back to sleep without a user present
Agents Week 2026 review: sandbox execution, private networking, and memory moved from framework code to network primitives
Dynamic Workflows: durable execution engine for multi-step applications with higher concurrency
Workers Paid plan includes Durable Objects usage for /month minimum
SQLite-backed Durable Objects storage billing enabled January 2026
30+ self-contained demo examples in official cloudflare/agents GitHub repository
Sandbox execution, private networking, and memory moved from framework code to network primitives
Dynamic Workers begin with almost no ambient authority (globalOutbound: null)
Capabilities granted explicitly through bindings — resource by resource
Execution Ladder: Tier 0 (Workspace/SQLite), Tier 1 (Dynamic Worker no network), Tier 2 (Dynamic Worker + npm)
worker-bundler fetches packages from npm registry and bundles with esbuild at runtime

stats.fact: Cloudflare internal AI engineering stack processed 20 million requests routed through AI Gateway during Agents Week
241 billion tokens processed through Cloudflare Workers AI (Agents Week 2026)
Dynamic Workers are ~100x faster and up to 100x more memory-efficient than traditional containers
Cloudflare Workers Paid plan: /month minimum includes Durable Objects, Workers, KV, and Hyperdrive
More than 3,683 internal Cloudflare users served inference on Workers AI during Agents Week
Project Think is in preview as of April 2026; APIs may change as feedback is incorporated
Facets (child Durable Objects) have RPC latency comparable to function calls, not HTTP round-trips

angles: Why actor-based durable execution beats stateless request-response for multi-step AI agents
Step-by-step guide: building your first Think-based agent with @cloudflare/think
The Execution Ladder explained: how agents progressively escalate to sandboxed code environments
Facets vs monolithic agents: designing modular multi-agent systems with colocated Durable Objects
Self-authored extensions: agents that create their own tools at runtime without redeployment
Project Think vs AWS Bedrock AgentCore vs Google ADK: architectural comparison
Production considerations: cost, hibernation, SQLite storage, and cron scheduling
outline: H2: What is Cloudflare Project Think?
H2: Core Primitives: Fibers, Facets, Sessions, and Sandboxed Execution
H2: Getting Started with the Think Base Class (@cloudflare/think)
H2: The Execution Ladder: Progressive Security for AI-Generated Code
H2: Building Modular Agents with Facets (Sub-Agents)
H2: Persistent Sessions: Tree-Structured Conversations and Branching
H2: Self-Authored Extensions: Agents That Build Their Own Tools
H2: Project Think vs AWS Bedrock AgentCore vs Google ADK
H2: Production Deployment: Cost, Scheduling, and Hibernation
